---
title: "Claude Code Block Demo"
---

This page demonstrates the custom Claude Code block styling. Use ` ```claude` to create styled command blocks.

## Basic Claude Commands

Simple commands without prompts:

```claude
/plugin marketplace add tidymodels/skills
```

```claude
what does this project do?
```

## Multi-line Commands

Commands with longer descriptions:

```claude
I have a CSV file with customer data and want to predict churn.
The dataset has 15 features including demographics, usage patterns,
and service history.
```

## Multiple Commands

```claude
/plugin marketplace add tidymodels/skills
/plugin install tidymodels-users@tidymodels-skills
```

## Comparison with Regular Code Blocks

Regular bash block:
```bash
cd my-project
claude
```

Claude command block:
```claude
what technologies does this project use?
```

Regular R code:
```r
library(tidymodels)
data(mtcars)
```

Claude command asking about R:
```claude
explain how the tidymodels ecosystem works
```

## Features

The `claude` code block styling includes:

- Dark terminal-inspired background
- "Claude Code" label in the top right
- Cranberry accent colors
- Enhanced copy button styling
- Subtle gradient and shadow effects
- Maintains consistency with site design

## Usage

Simply use ` ```claude` instead of ` ``` ` for any Claude Code commands or prompts in your documentation.
